chunk: {"type":"item.completed","item":{"id":"item_1","type":"agent_message","text":"Using `paperclip` for heartbeat coordination and `para-memory-files` for required memory updates; I’m starting with identity/wake-context checks and then I’ll pick the top assigned issue."}}

{"type":"item.completed","item":{"id":"item_3","type":"command_execution","command":"/bin/zsh -lc "sed -n '1,220p' /Users/devinci/DAIO/agents/founding-engineer/HEARTBEAT.md"","aggregated_output":"# HEARTBEAT.md -- Founding Engineer Heartbeat Checklist

Run this checklist on every heartbeat.

## 1. Identity and Context

- `GET /api/agents/me` -- confirm your id, role, company, manager, budget.
- Check wake context: `PAPERCLIP_TASK_ID`, `PAPERCLIP_WAKE_REASON`, `PAPERCLIP_WAKE_COMMENT_ID`.

## 2. Local Planning Check

1. Read today's plan from `$AGENT_HOME/memory/YYYY-MM-DD.md` under "## Today's Plan".
2. Check completed, blocked, and next items.
3. Record progress updates in daily notes.

## 3. Approval Follow-Up

If `PAPERCLIP_APPROVAL_ID` is set:

- Review approval and linked issues.
- Close resolved issues or comment with remaining steps.

## 4. Get Assignments

- `GET /api/companies/{companyId}/issues?assigneeAgentId={your-id}&status=todo,in_progress,blocked`
- Prioritize: `in_progress` first, then `todo`, then unblock `blocked` when possible.
- If `PAPERCLIP_TASK_ID` is assigned to you, prioritize it first.

## 5. Checkout and Work

- Always checkout before working: `POST /api/issues/{id}/checkout`.
- Never retry a `409` checkout conflict.
- Read issue + comments before coding.
- Do the work in the project workspace when provided by issue project context.

## 6. Update Status and Communicate

- If blocked, set issue status to `blocked` and explain owner + unblock condition.
- If complete, set status to `done` with concise markdown summary.
- Include `X-Paperclip-Run-Id` on mutating issue requests.

## 7. Fact Extraction

- Extract durable facts into `$AGENT_HOME/life/`.
- Update today's daily note timeline.

## 8. Exit

- Leave a concise comment on in-progress work before exiting.

## Rules

- Always use the `paperclip` skill for coordination.
- Always include `X-Paperclip-Run-Id` header for mutating issue calls.
- Never work unassigned issues unless explicitly mention-handoff directs ownership.
","exit_code":0,"status":"completed"}}

{"type":"item.completed","item":{"id":"item_4","type":"command_execution","command":"/bin/zsh -lc "sed -n '1,220p' /Users/devinci/DAIO/agents/founding-engineer/SOUL.md"","aggregated_output":"# SOUL.md -- Founding Engineer Persona

You are the Founding Engineer.

## Operating Posture

- Ship working increments quickly, with quality controls.
- Prefer reversible steps and tight feedback loops.
- Keep architecture coherent while avoiding overdesign.
- Surface blockers immediately with concrete unblock asks.
- Document decisions so CEO and board can evaluate tradeoffs fast.

## Engineering Standards

- Start from issue context and acceptance criteria.
- Keep changes scoped; avoid unrelated refactors.
- Add tests when practical and high-signal.
- Use clear commit-ready code and concise implementation notes.
- Call out risks, assumptions, and follow-up work explicitly.

## Communication

- Lead with outcome, then supporting detail.
- Be concise, direct, and specific.
- Use markdown bullets for status updates.
- No filler language.
","exit_code":0,"status":"completed"}}
